First Season · Spring 2026

My Garden
Plan

Planters · Raised Beds · Pots · Trees

🌡️ Loading weather...
01

Container Overview

🌿 Corner Trellis Planter
Right-angle triangle — 70 × 70 cm trellis sides
Cherry tomatoes (Side A, vertical) + Beef tomatoes (Side B, horizontal). Marigolds & nasturtiums along the long (diagonal) side.
view diagram →
🌿 Planter 1
20 × 90 × 25 cm
Herbs & spring onions: basil (in grow house until warm), parsley, chives, spring onions. Mint on hold for next season.
view diagram →
🥗 Planter 2
20 × 90 × 25 cm
Fully salad & lettuce: mixed salad leaves (cut & come again), Little Gem lettuce, rocket. Sow successionally.
view diagram →
🥬 Raised Bed 1
60 × 120 cm — curved edges
Perennial kale centre · lettuce & spinach inner ring · purple curly kale outer ring · nasturtium border.
view diagram →
🌱 Raised Bed 2
60 × 120 cm — curved edges
Asparagus centre (permanent) · pak choi & tenderstem broccoli inner ring · spring onions outer ring · nasturtium border.
view diagram →
🥔 Grow Bag 1
40–50 L
Potatoes #1 — currently chitting. Maris Piper or Charlotte.
view diagram →
🥔 Grow Bag 2
40–50 L
Potatoes #2 — second bag for larger yield. Maris Piper or Charlotte.
view diagram →
🥕 Grow Bag 3
40–50 L
Carrots — sow directly in rows. Nantes 2 or Chantenay Red Cored.
view diagram →
🌿 Grow Bag 4
Large (40–50 L+)
Fig — transfer from patio pot as season progresses to allow a bigger tree. Move to shed in hard frosts.
view diagram →
🪴 Individual Pots
7–20 L each
Aubergine, chillies (7–10 L, overwinter indoors), courgette (20 L, annual).
view diagram →
🍁 Japanese Maple
≥ 40 L pot
Fully hardy — leave outside year-round. 'Bloodgood' or 'Sango-kaku'.
view diagram →
🍓 Strawberries
Individual pots
Strawberries in individual pots — growhouse in hard frosts.
view diagram →
⏸️ On Hold
Future seasons
Blueberries, olive, bay, mulberry, mint. Fruit trees pending garden layout (swing, seating etc.)
Deferred
02

Planting Diagrams

Corner Trellis Planter

right-angle triangle · 70 × 70 cm
trellis side (70 cm)long side — flowers🍅 tomato plant position
Right-angle triangle planter with 70 cm trellis on each of the two right-angle sides. Marigolds & nasturtiums planted along the long (diagonal) side as companion plants. Pinch out side shoots weekly. Feed weekly once flowering begins.

Planter 1 — Herbs & Spring Onions

20 × 90 cm
🌿
Basil
In grow house — needs warmth
🌱
Parsley
Hardy & reliable
🌾
Chives
Snip & regrow
🧅
Spring Onions
Sow every 3 weeks
Basil kept in grow house to avoid cold snaps — move to planter once reliably warm. Mint on hold — add next season in a pot-in-pot to contain roots.

Planter 2 — Salad & Lettuce

20 × 90 cm
🥗
Mixed Salad Leaves
Cut & come again
🥬
Little Gem Lettuce
Compact · sow successionally
🌿
Rocket
Spicy · quick to harvest
Keep seedlings indoors until mid–late April. Harden off gradually before transplanting. Sow small batches every 2–3 weeks for a continuous harvest.

Raised Bed 1 — Vegetables

60 × 120 cm · curved edges
🥬🥬
Perennial
Kale ×2
Lettuce
Spinach
🌸 Nasturtiums (border)
🌸 Nasturtiums (border)
Purple
Curly Kale
Purple
Curly Kale
Perennial kale = permanent centre, do not disturb. Purple curly kale — sow May–Jun per seed packet for Sep harvest. Nasturtiums trail beautifully over curved edges.

Raised Bed 2 — Asparagus & Brassicas

60 × 120 cm · curved edges
🌱🌱
Asparagus
(permanent)
Pak
Choi
Tenderstem
Broccoli
🌸 Nasturtiums (border)
🌸 Nasturtiums (border)
Spring
Onions
Spring
Onions
No asparagus harvest in year one — patience pays off from year two! Tenderstem broccoli at the back of inner ring as it grows tall. All brassicas need enviromesh protection.

Grow Bags

40–50 L each
🥔
Potatoes #1
Maris Piper or Charlotte
Currently chitting on windowsill. Half fill bag; earth up as shoots reach 15–20cm.
🥔
Potatoes #2
Maris Piper or Charlotte
Second potato bag — chit alongside bag #1. Earth up as shoots emerge.
🥕
Carrots
Nantes 2 or Chantenay
Sow directly in rows from late March. Fill fully with light compost + grit. Thin to 5cm.
🌿
Fig
Move in as season progresses
Transfer fig to a large grow bag as the season progresses to allow a bigger tree to develop. Move to shed in hard frosts.

Individual Pots

🍆
Aubergine
~7–10 L
Move indoors winter
🌶️
Habanero Chilli
~7–10 L
Move indoors winter
Can last multiple years!
🥒
Courgette
~20 L
Annual
🍁
Japanese Maple
≥40 L
Fully Hardy
'Bloodgood' or 'Sango-kaku'
🍓
Strawberries ×5
Individual pots
Hardy
Growhouse in hard frosts
03

Plant Database

Click a plant name to see its full overview — container, temperature guide, and pest advice. Click the stage badge to advance its growth stage.
CropStageNext StepPlacementActions
🍅 Cherry TomatoesThin/prick out at 2 true leavesCorner trellis — Side A
🍅 Beef Tomatoes (heirloom)Thin/prick out at 2 true leavesCorner trellis — Side B
🍆 AubergineThin/prick out at 2 true leavesPot (~7–10 L) moveable
🌶️ Habanero ChilliesThin/prick out at 2–3 true leavesPot (~7–10 L) moveable
🌿 BasilThin lightly; separate if crowdedPlanter 1
🌱 ParsleyThin lightlyPlanter 1
🌼 MarigoldsThin or separateCorner trellis border
🥗 Mixed LettuceKeep indoors until mid–late April; harden offPlanter 2
🥬 Purple Curly KaleWatch for slugs; fleece if coldRaised Bed 1 outer ring
🥦 Tenderstem BroccoliWatch for slugs carefully!Raised Bed 2 inner ring
🌸 NasturtiumsFrost sensitive — cover if neededRaised Bed 1 & 2 borders
🍓 StrawberriesProtect from frostSeparate pots; growhouse
🌿 FigKeep south-facing; shed in hard frostPatio pot — moveable
🥔 Seed PotatoesPlant when shoots reach 1–2cmGrow Bag 1
04

Supplies & Protection

Supplies Progress0 / 10 items
🛡️ Pest Protection
Wool Pellets
—
Put down NOW before seedlings emerge. Slugs are active!
Hoop Frame / Piping
8–10mm alkathene pipe
Support netting over both raised beds
Bird Netting
Fine gauge, 2–3m wide roll
Over raised beds + strawberry pots
Copper Tape
Self-adhesive, min 3cm wide
Around all pot and planter rims
Fine Insect Mesh
Enviromesh
Over brassicas in Raised Bed 2 — essential!
Neem Oil Spray
Organic
Aphid control without harming ladybirds
🪴 Compost & Supplies
Peat-Free Compost
Multi-purpose
For all general pots and planters
Horticultural Grit
—
Mix into compost for drainage in pots and grow bags
Slow-Release Fertiliser
Granules
Add to pots and raised beds in spring
Garden Fleece
—
Cover raised beds now to warm soil and protect seedlings
05

Pest & Bird Protection

🪶
Birds
  • Hoop frames over raised beds with bird netting — easy to lift for harvesting
  • Individual pot covers for strawberries — net the moment fruit forms
  • Shiny tape or old CDs offer some deterrence but netting is the only reliable method
🐌
Slugs & Snails
  • Wool pellets around seedlings NOW — before they even emerge is ideal timing
  • Copper tape around all pot and planter rims
  • Sharp grit or crushed eggshell around base of plants
  • Hand-pick at dusk with a torch after rain — very effective
  • Avoid straw mulch — creates perfect hiding spots for slugs
  • No slug pellets — harmful to hedgehogs and wildlife
🐛
Aphids
  • Strong blast of water to knock aphids off — simple and effective
  • Neem oil spray — organic, disrupts lifecycle without harming ladybirds
  • Marigolds attract aphid predators — already in trellis planter ✅
  • Nasturtiums as trap crop — aphids prefer them over your veg ✅
  • Brassicas in Raised Bed 2 are aphid magnets — watch closely!
Crop / BedBird ProtectionSlug & SnailAphids
Raised Bed 1Hoop + bird nettingWool pellets + gritNeem oil + water blast
Raised Bed 2 ⚠️Hoop + netting + enviromeshWool pellets + gritNeem oil — brassicas are aphid magnets!
Trellis PlanterLess of a concernCopper tape on planterMarigolds + nasturtiums + neem oil
StrawberriesPot covers / fruit cageCopper tape on potsWater blast
Planters 1 & 2Less of a concernCopper tape on plantersWater blast
Grow BagsLess of a concernCopper tape on edgesWater blast
06

Temperature Guide

🌿 Basil
Min: 15°C nights
Keep indoors until late May
🍆 Aubergine
Min: 10°C
Overwinter indoors or growhouse
🌶️ Chillies
Min: 10°C
Overwinter indoors — lasts years!
🍅 Tomatoes
Min: 10°C
Annual; remove after first frost
🥒 Courgette
Min: 10°C
Annual; remove after first frost
🫒 Olive
Min: -5°C
Move under cover in hard frosts
🌿 Bay
Min: -10°C
Shelter in prolonged hard frosts
🌿 Fig
Min: -10°C
Wrap or move to shed in hard frosts
🫐 Blueberries
Min: -15°C
Leave outside year-round
🍁 Japanese Maple
Min: -20°C
Leave outside; shelter from wind
🍓 Strawberries
Min: -15°C
Growhouse in severe frost only
🌱 Asparagus
Min: -20°C
Leave in bed; dies back in winter
🥬 Kale (all)
Min: -15°C
Leave in bed; very frost tolerant
🥕 Carrots
Min: -10°C
Leave in grow bag; fleece in hard frosts
07

Seasonal Timeline

🌱 March — Now
Fleece over both raised beds · put wool pellets down now · continue chitting potatoes · sow courgette & spinach indoors · keep lettuce seedlings indoors · plant asparagus crowns into Raised Bed 2 centre
🌿 Late March
Sow carrots directly into Grow Bag 2 · buy and plant perennial kale (Daubenton ×2) into Raised Bed 1 centre · check on direct sown seeds — fleece on if cold snap forecast
🌸 April
Harden off lettuce — outside during the day, inside at night · sow pak choi and spring onions · start hardening tomatoes, aubergine, chillies on mild days · watch for slugs as temperatures rise
☀️ Late April / May
Transplant lettuce into Planter 2 · plant tomatoes out into corner trellis planter · plant aubergine and chillies into their pots after last frost · sow purple curly kale per seed packet
🍅 May Onwards
Begin weekly tomato feeds once flowering · earth up potatoes regularly · net strawberries the moment fruit starts forming · watch brassicas closely for cabbage white butterflies · enjoy the garden! 🌿
☀️ June
Harvest early strawberries · continue earthing up potatoes · deadhead marigolds to keep flowering · begin harvesting cut-and-come-again salad leaves · transfer fig to large grow bag for stronger root growth
🌻 July
Harvest first cherry tomatoes · pick courgettes regularly to encourage more fruits · harvest pak choi before it bolts · continue weekly tomato feeds · harvest carrots as needed · water daily in hot spells
🍂 August
Harvest main crop potatoes · pick beef tomatoes as they ripen · sow green manure in any empty beds · harvest and dry chillies or freeze them · check fig for ripening fruit
🍁 September
Harvest purple curly kale · sow overwintering salad leaves (spinach, land cress) · clear finished tomato plants · sow green manure or cover beds with fleece · begin hardening off chillies for overwintering indoors
🌧️ October
Move aubergine and chilli pots indoors before first frost · mulch raised beds with compost · cut back asparagus fronds once they yellow · move fig to shed ahead of cold nights
❄️ November – December
Keep raised beds mulched and covered with fleece · check overwintering chillies and aubergine indoors (minimal watering) · begin chitting next season's potatoes in late December · plan next year's planting and order seeds
🪴 My Garden Plan · First Season 2026 · Made with care
